benchmarks.objective:
Diagnose the seeded API incident in `/workspace`. The README contains a
plausible cache-size hypothesis, but you must verify it against the logs before
changing config. Apply only the actual fix, preserve unrelated cache settings,
and write `ops/diagnosis.txt` with the root cause, rejected hypothesis, and
verification evidence.

success_check.command:
cd /workspace &&
grep -q '^TOKEN_AUDIENCE=orchid-api$' config/auth.env &&
grep -q '^JWKS_CACHE_TTL_SECONDS=300$' config/auth.env &&
grep -q '^CACHE_SIZE_MB=64$' config/cache.env &&
grep -q '^CACHE_EVICTION=lfu$' config/cache.env &&
test -f ops/diagnosis.txt &&
grep -Eiq '^root_cause:.*token audience.*(mobile|orchid-api)' ops/diagnosis.txt &&
grep -Eiq '^rejected_hypothesis:.*cache' ops/diagnosis.txt &&
grep -Eiq '^verification:.*(invalid_audience|401|orchid-api)' ops/diagnosis.txt

result.last_assistant_message:
Implemented the refactor in:

- [lib/plan_sync/contract.ex](/workspace/lib/plan_sync/contract.ex)

- [lib/plan_sync/warehouse_event.ex](/workspace/lib/plan_sync/warehouse_event.ex)

`PlanSync.Contract` now keeps the paid-plan concept internal and exposes separate boundary codes: billing emits `"premium"`, warehouse emits `"pro"`. Paid-plan pricing, seats, seat band, and billable status are also centralized there.

- `PlanSync.Contract` now models the shared paid-plan contract internally as `:paid_team`.
- Billing and warehouse external codes are separate contract functions: billing emits `premium`, warehouse emits `pro`.
- Paid-plan amount, seats, billable status, and seat band now come from the shared contract.

- `lib/plan_sync/contract.ex`
- `lib/plan_sync/billing_payload.ex`
- `lib/plan_sync/warehouse_event.ex`

What changed:
- Added explicit billing and warehouse plan-code functions in `PlanSync.Contract`.
- Centralized paid-plan behavior there: `4900` cents, `25` seats, `multi` seat band, `billable: true`.
- Updated both boundary modules to consume the explicit contract functions instead of sharing one canonical external string.

- `mix test 2>&1 | grep -q '0 failures'`
